items.prompt: What does Example 8.13 emphasize about documentation depth?
choices: Every dataset needs identical page counts
Intended use (for example training versus exploration) should drive detail level
Documentation is optional for machine learning
items.explain: Purpose and audience determine how much detail each documentation component needs.

items.prompt: In the FAIR principles, “Findable” most directly requires:
choices: Persistent identifiers and rich discovery metadata
items.explain: Findability depends on identifiers, titles, and metadata that support discovery.

items.prompt: Markdown documentation can include overview, dictionary, and variable descriptions and works well with version control.
items.explain: Example 8.15 and Section 8.3 present Markdown as a maintainable human-readable format.

items.prompt: Name three sections from the retail documentation template in Example 8.19.
items.rubric: Any three of overview, metadata, data dictionary, provenance, notes.

items.prompt: A pre-release review by someone who did not create the dataset helps verify interpretability and reproducibility of the docs.
items.explain: Independent review checks whether outsiders can interpret variables and reproduce the processing path.
